marketName: Port Electrification — Shore Power, Crane Smoothing, Ferry & Terminal-Vehicle Charging

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of undersizing BESS/microgrid capacity by >20% relative to AFIR-2030 peak OPS demand (cruise up to 16 MW, container ~7.5 MW per berth) due to incomplete vessel-call-profile data
Increase the ability to produce a bankable multi-revenue model combining AFIR-compliance value, peak-shaving (§19 StromNEV), HPC buffering and PV firming in a single port-wide sizing study
Minimize the likelihood of missing the CEF-Transport / AFIR co-funding window (up to 50% CAPEX for TEN-T ports) during initial business-case definition
Minimize the likelihood of discovering a DSO MV-capacity blocker after tender-award milestone, causing >12-month AFIR-compliance slip
Increase the ability to secure a buildable 40-80 m2 per MWh quayside footprint with Feuerwehr access that does not impede terminal throughput or ISPS-restricted zones
Minimize the likelihood of siting selections that violate IEC 60721-3 salt-air corrosion class (C5-M / CX coastal) requiring container re-hardening post-tender
Minimize the time-to-Ready-to-Build from tender award to under 15 months to meet AFIR 2030 milestone under public-procurement schedule
Minimize variability between planned and as-built BoP scope (cable runs, MV transformer, OPS reel interface) causing >8% cost overrun on a fixed-price public tender
Minimize the likelihood of schedule slip driven by Feuerwehr/VdS fire-protection clearance re-design for Li-ion container adjacency to passenger and cruise berths
Increase the ability to respond to VOB/VgV public tenders with a compliant, scored-above-threshold bid package despite zero prior public-port references
Minimize the likelihood of OPS-interface mis-handover between landside (VDE-AR-N 4110) and shipside (IEC/IEEE 80005-1 HVSC) boundary responsibilities
Minimize the likelihood of commissioning holdup caused by firmware-version incompatibility between BMS, PCS, EMS and port-side SCADA / TOS uplinks
Minimize the time to close VDE-AR-N 4110 and IEC 80005-1 witness-test non-conformities to under 30 days from finding during a live berth window
Maximize the share of AFIR-scope vessel calls that receive OPS connection within the berth window at contracted power to above 95%
Minimize the likelihood of exceeding the contracted DSO demand-charge ceiling during simultaneous OPS + STS-crane + HPC peaks by more than 5% per month
Minimize the likelihood of PCS trip under grid-fault ride-through while an AFIR-scope ship is on OPS, which would force vessel auxiliary-engine restart
Minimize the likelihood of undetected salt-air-induced capacity fade or enclosure corrosion exceeding warranty threshold before the annual availability audit
Minimize dispatch-priority drift between OPS, HPC and peak-shaving queues under concurrent port events to under 1% missed-setpoint per week
Minimize the operator effort to commission an additional OPS berth or HPC dispenser onto the existing port microgrid without re-tendering the whole asset
Minimize the likelihood that adding new vessel classes (LNG dual-fuel, methanol, battery-hybrid) requires re-certification of the shoreside OPS interface beyond parameter tuning
Minimize the effort to execute annual port-authority and DSO re-certification audits through a pre-packaged single-evidence dossier
Minimize the likelihood of end-of-life Li-ion disposal non-compliance with EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 reporting obligations under public-asset ownership
Increase the ability to present the BESS-microgrid CAPEX as a co-funded, AFIR-compliant, future-proof port asset with committed multi-revenue evidence at the supervisory-board review
Minimize the likelihood of public-tender audit findings or Feuerwehr refusals that publicly signal immature engineering in a politically visible port project
Minimize the risk perception of missing the AFIR-2030 compliance window by ensuring a single EPC counterparty owns the full schedule including DSO, IEC 80005-1 ship interface and VdS fire clearance
Minimize the likelihood of accelerated cycle-life degradation from sustained high C-rate (ferry fast-charge, STS regen) exceeding LFP 0.5 C design point
Minimize the likelihood of MV protection-relay mis-coordination between DSO feeder, terminal ring-bus and OPS ship-side breakers causing nuisance trips during fault ride-through
Minimize the time to recover contracted SoC window after a high-C-rate cruise-berth discharge event before the next vessel call
